title: Infinite loop caused by item linking in nested loops
symptom: The workflow enters an infinite loop after processing the first batch, leading to thousands of executions despite a limited number of JSON files.
applies_when: Processing items in a nested loop with a Code node
not_this_if: Only one item is returned from the second loop node
root_cause: When using nested loops, if more items than expected are returned from the inner loop, the outer loop will treat these as new items to process, causing an infinite loop. This happens due to how item linking works in n8n.
fix_steps: Modify the second loop node to return only one item to the first loop node.
Consider using an Aggregate node between the loops to ensure only one item is returned.
Alternatively, use a subworkflow to separate the executions.
agent_instruction: Ensure only one item is returned from the second loop node to prevent infinite looping.
